it.skip — real ally damage buff but chained through a heal event (43 normals x20 stacks -> heal) the sim has no primitive/pool for

fixtures: controlComp('ada'). Crown is the built-in B2 of the control comp (liter B1 / crown B2 / carry B3 / helm B3, boss Fire), so she is NOT injected — I read her emitted buffApply events off a run with a simSupported carry (ada). Crown casts B2 every rotation so both her burst block and skill1 (FB-enter) fire; controlComp supplies B1/B2 so Full Bursts actually occur. crownSlot self-identified from her unique buff magnitudes (64.51/44.35/37.44/36.24), no index-field dependency. Counterfactuals via withPatchedOverride('crown', ...) zero one crown stat at a time and re-run.
gaps: burst Shield (10.45% caster Max HP): it.skip — no 'shield' kind in cfg.onEvent and no HP pool; offensively inert and unobservable via the event API.

skill2 'when recovery takes effect' Attack Damage ▲ 20.99% (7s, all allies): it.skip — a genuine ally damage buff, but its trigger chains through the Relax heal-stack machine (43 normal attacks/stack x20 -> max-stack heal -> on-recovery) and the sim models no heal pool/primitive to fire it; measurement/primitive-gated.
skill2 Relax healing stacks + max-stack invuln/taunt/self-heal: defensive, no offensive primitive.
